science.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
science.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

science.title: Scientists Discover Genetic “Off Switch” That Supercharges CAR T Cells Against Cancer
science.summary: A new study reveals a possible way to make CAR T-cell therapy more durable and effective by targeting a single gene-regulating protein. CAR T-cell therapy is widely seen as a breakthrough in personalized cancer care. The treatment works by modifying a patient’s own immune cells so they can identify and attack cancer ce

science.title: New quantum sensor could count individual photons and hunt dark matter
science.summary: Researchers have built an ultra-sensitive sensor capable of detecting unimaginably small amounts of energy — below one zeptojoule. The breakthrough relies on fragile superconducting materials that react to even the slightest temperature change. This level of precision could improve quantum computers, enable photon coun

science.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
science.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it onl

science.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
science.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researc

tech.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
tech.summary: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

tech.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
tech.summary: Hugging Face debuts $2,500 bipedal robot project for builders and researchers.

tech.title: Dutch government blocks US company from acquisition, citing ‘risk to public interest’
tech.summary: The move to block the acquisition of the cloud company that hosts the Dutch digital ID service comes as Europe continues to reduce its reliance on U.S. technology.

tech.title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
tech.summary: Stord offers a network of physical warehouses and inventory management software for e-commerce. It bills itself as a sort of anti-Amazon, giving brands "the speed to compete" while still owning their customer relationships.

tech.title: I Spent a Week Recording Myself Doing Chores for Money. Who's the Robot Now?
tech.summary: Cooking. Doing laundry. Tidying up. All your household tasks can be turned into data to train future humanoids—if you’re prepared for the consequences.

medicine.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
medicine.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

medicine.title: Breakthrough drug reverses aging in skin and dramatically speeds healing
medicine.summary: Scientists have discovered that a topical anti-aging drug called ABT-263 can dramatically improve wound healing in older skin. The treatment works by removing damaged “senescent” cells that accumulate with age and slow the body’s repair process. In aged mice, wounds healed much faster after treatment, while the drug al

medicine.title: People who lost the most weight on Ozempic saw huge health benefits
medicine.summary: People who lost significant weight while taking Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, or Saxenda had sharply lower risks of major obesity-related health problems, including sleep apnea and kidney disease. Those who gained weight instead faced higher risks — especially for heart failure — even though many patients discontinued the

medicine.title: Blocking two cancer pathways may curb medulloblastoma relapse, preclinical study suggests
medicine.summary: For most children diagnosed with medulloblastoma, the most common malignant pediatric brain tumor, survival rates are encouraging. But for a subset, remission is not the end of the story. Roughly 30% of patients will see their cancer return, and once it does, outcomes are often devastating.

medicine.title: Vitamin D analog shuts down pancreatic cancer's shield in a clinical trial
medicine.summary: A small clinical trial led by Dana-Farber Cancer Institute researchers has put a Salk Institute idea to the test in patients: that activating the vitamin D receptor can help reshape the protective environment surrounding pancreatic tumors in ways that could make the notoriously difficult-to-treat cancer more vulnerable

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics
mathematics.summary: Grothendieck is revered in the world of math; outside of it, he’s known for his unusual life, if he’s known at all. But what were his actual mathematical contributions? The post How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean?
mathematics.summary: At 25, Kurt Gödel proved there can never be a mathematical “theory of everything.” Columnist Natalie Wolchover explores the implications. The post What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Unknowable Math Can Help Hide Secrets
mathematics.summary: A graduate student recently harnessed the complexity of mathematical proofs to create a powerful new tool in cryptography. The post How Unknowable Math Can Help Hide Secrets first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
space.summary: Starship V3 led to some stunning launch photography after it lifted off from Starbase on SpaceX's 12th flight of the rocket that aims to land astronauts on the moon.

space.title: This cryptocurrency billionaire will fly SpaceX's 1st private Starship to Mars, but when?
space.summary: SpaceX says it will launch a private flyby mission to Mars that will be led by cryptocurrency billionaire Chun Wang, but when it will fly is unclear.

space.title: Seeing double with the Artemis 2 rocket boosters | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
space.summary: The two boosters hang out mid-air after separation.

space.title: 'Very interesting wiggles' in data from silent NASA Mars spacecraft lead to unexpected solar wind discovery
space.summary: Data from NASA's MAVEN spacecraft revealed a never-before-seen atmospheric effect on Mars, revealing how solar storms may shape planets without strong magnetic fields.

space.title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
space.summary: DARPA has a plan to keep geosynchronous satellites going for years beyond their expiration date. The agency aims to test it with a mission launching as soon as this summer.

astronomy.title: How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form?
astronomy.summary: A planetary odd couple — a mini-Neptune and a hot Jupiter — probably formed much farther away from their star before migrating closer in. The post How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31
astronomy.summary: Venus and Jupiter — the Evening Star and the False Evening Star — draw closer together in the western twilight and point down to Mercury. The waxing Moon shows off its rich telescopic detail. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: The Definitive Census of Multiple Star Systems Within 10 Parsecs
astronomy.summary: Our Sun is a loner. It lacks a stellar companion hurtling through interstellar space with it. But we’ve known for a long time that’s actually relatively rare - most stars have at least one gravitationally bound partner. Understanding how exactly those stars are related to each other is critical for observational campai

astronomy.title: Is Dust the Best Thing in the Universe? Part 4: We Owe Dust Our Lives
astronomy.summary: No dust, no way to cool a collapsing gas cloud. No way to cool it, no stars. No dust, no first rung on the ladder from grain to pebble to planet. The substance I spent two articles complaining about turns out to be the substance that makes me possible.

astronomy.title: A Beautiful Death: How a Dying Star Created the Crystal Ball Nebula
astronomy.summary: Planetary nebula are created when a dying star sheds it outer layers. The gas is lit up by the star and all the gorgeous, changing detail is exposed. architecture.summary: The Municipal Environmental School 15 de Outubro Mayor Firmino Filho, established in the Northern Zone of Teresina (PI), is integrated into the João Mendes Olímpio de Melo City Park and serves as an educational facility closely linked to the territory. architecture.title: MAC Panamá Selects Palma + Taller TO to Design Its New Museum Building
architecture.summary: Following an international design competition launched in January 2026, the Museum of Contemporary Art of Panama (MAC Panamá) announced the selection of Mexican architects Palma + Taller TO to design its new building. architecture.title: Dezeen Debate features "proper dystopian" Prairie Ark Gallery
architecture.summary: The latest edition of our Dezeen Debate newsletter features the Prairie Ark gallery by Chinese architecture studio Büro Ziyu Zhuang, which resembles a flying saucer. architecture.title: Translucent Centre Pompidou Hanwha set to open in Seoul
architecture.summary: The Centre Pompidou Hanwha designed by French architecture studio Wilmotte & Associés has been revealed ahead of its opening in Seoul, South Korea, next month. Set to open in Seoul's financial district on 4 June, the four-storey building contains two large galleries alongside a cafe, shop, restaurant and auditorium

architecture.title: Kengo Kuma & Associates designs its first museum building in the US for Pennsylvania
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Kengo Kuma & Associates and landscape design office Field Operations have released designs for a vernacular expansion to the Brandywine Conservancy & Museum of Art outside of Philadelphia, USA. construction.title: The top commercial contractors of 2026: ENR
construction.summary: Turner Construction remained No. 1 for another year, while Mortenson leapt 12 spots to crack the top 10 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 list.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Oldham Council set to launch £20M highways framework
construction.summary: Oldham Metropolitan Borough Council has issued a pipeline notice for a potential £20M highways improvement framework covering civil engineering construction works across north east Greater Manchester. The post Oldham Council set to launch £20M highways framework appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: CCS launches £120bn construction and infrastructure framework
construction.summary: The Crown Commercial Service (CCS) - the trading fund and executive agency of the Cabinet Office - has launched procurement for a major new construction and infrastructure framework, with an estimated value of £120bn excluding VAT (£144bn including VAT). ai.summary: Parameter Golf brought together 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions to explore AI-assisted machine learning research, coding agents, quantization, and novel model design under strict constraints.
